title: Atorvastatin Generic Guidance
content: Lipitor is a brand for atorvastatin. Generic atorvastatin is therapeutically equivalent for most statin use cases and commonly has lower patient out-of-pocket cost.

title: Rosuvastatin Cost Check
content: Crestor is rosuvastatin brand. Generic rosuvastatin is often preferred by plans due to lower pharmacy spend with similar LDL-lowering efficacy.

title: Insulin Lispro Equivalent
content: Humalog and unbranded insulin lispro are clinically comparable in most dosing protocols. Formulary preference can significantly change patient copay.

title: DPP-4 Formulary Review
content: Januvia can be expensive under some plans. Plan-preferred DPP-4 options and manufacturer savings programs should be checked to reduce cost burden.

title: Anticoagulant Formulary Strategy
content: Eliquis may require prior authorization. If cost is high, compare formulary anticoagulants and generic availability where approved.

title: Brand Medication Cost Flag
content: Brand-only medication billing above market references should be reviewed for generic substitution, therapeutic interchange, and 90-day supply optimization.

title: Duplicate Charge Check
content: Repeated medication lines with similar names and close pricing can indicate duplicate billing entries and should be validated against administration records.

title: Clinical Safety Note
content: Any suggested medication switch must be confirmed by a licensed clinician. Cost optimization should not override clinical contraindications.
